diagnosis.node_dns: registry-1.docker.io returns SERVFAIL via Docker/k3d node resolver; CoreDNS service is running but external Docker Hub DNS is unreliable from cluster/node path
diagnosis.builder_pod_dns: ClusterFirst DNS could not resolve host.k3d.internal; builder originally failed on registry-1.docker.io and then on host.k3d.internal
diagnosis.local_registry_reachability: node can reach existing registry over http://172.18.0.1:5000; python:3.10-slim mirror is present at /v2/mirror/dockerhub/library/python/tags/list
diagnosis.failure_class: builder substrate DNS/registry resolution, not AMOF source truth or gmd app bug

fix_chosen.approach: local registry mirror plus local-build base image rewrite and builder BuildKit insecure registry config
fix_chosen.why: keeps fix local-only and builder-specific; avoids broad CoreDNS/network surgery

images.name: amof-assistant
images.skip_reason: Assistant build is opt-in (set AMOF_REQUIRE_ASSISTANT=1 to enable). Not part of the cloud-dev happy path.

retry_result.base_image_resolution: restored: build loaded metadata and layers for 172.18.0.1:5000/mirror/dockerhub/library/python:3.10-slim

new_blocker.classification: builder outbound package repository DNS/network blocker
new_blocker.message: apt-get update cannot resolve deb.debian.org, so curl/git packages cannot be installed in the controlplane image build

environment_truth.runtime_healthy_remains_truthful: gmd-dev Argo/runtime health can remain healthy independently of builder substrate readiness
environment_truth.projection_recommendation: later UI should distinguish runtime healthy from build substrate ready/not ready
